response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile software developer with strong web, backend, and some systems-level experience, but their profile does not match this role's core requirements. Their C++ exposure is limited to a brief 7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project, whereas the role demands deep, current C++/STL expertise. There is no evidence of industrial automation, IEC 62443/SDL security practices, MQTT/IoT protocols, or message-broker architecture at the level required. Adjacent strengths in low-latency DLang systems and event-driven platforms partially offset this, but not enough for a senior C++-focused industrial role.
strengths: Strong systems-performance background: low-latency, async I/O, high-throughput services in DLang at Dunnhumby
Broad engineering versatility with event-driven architecture experience (GISCollective geo-data platform)
Solid engineering craft: TDD, CI/CD, testing culture, mentoring via workshops and courses
concerns: Minimal and outdated C++ experience (~7 months in 2014–2015) versus a role centered on hands-on C++/STL development
No industrial automation, IEC 62443, SDL, or OT/industrial protocol (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA) experience
Recent career trajectory is web/full-stack (TypeScript/Node), diverging from embedded/industrial platform engineering

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity or IoT protocol background
Low-latency, concurrent systems experience in DLang
